approach_rationale: Smallest patch (1-2 lines) inside ShoppingListService.can_merge (mealie/services/household_services/shopping_lists.py:45-71) and/or ShoppingListService.merge_items (:73-128) to revert one of the two canonical bug-injection variants from input.md:88-128 (Variant A: line 96 += -> =, Variant B: can_merge line 52/71 keyed on display). Strict match for input intent which forbids surrounding refactors, schema changes, and toggles. Lowest blast radius; preserves all pre-existing green tests in test_group_shopping_lists.py, test_group_shopping_list_items.py, and test_group_mealplan.py. See approach/selected.md.
baseline_reality: The checked-out Mealie baseline at C:\Users\v-liyuanjun\Downloads\mealie\ does NOT exhibit the bug (verified: can_merge line 52 already rejects different food_id; line 71 is bool(item1.food_id) or item1.note == item2.note; merge_items line 96 already uses to_item.quantity += from_item.quantity). Per input.md:88-138, the operator MUST apply Variant A or Variant B on an inject-bug branch before running the case-3 working branch. SC-1 'FAIL before fix' therefore means 'FAIL on the bug-injected branch', not 'FAIL on canonical baseline'. v2 addresses ARCH-C-001 / ARCH-C-002 by surfacing this precondition.
problem_statement: When a user schedules the same recipe in multiple meal-plan slots and triggers 'Add Meal Plan to Shopping List', the resulting shopping list must contain exactly one row per (food_id, unit_id) whose quantity equals the recipe's per-ingredient quantity times the number of occurrences. With the injected bug present (Variant A at merge_items line 96 or Variant B at can_merge line 52/71), this invariant is violated either by lost accumulation (single-recipe quantity shown) or lost consolidation (multiple unmerged rows). Defect lives in ShoppingListService.can_merge or ShoppingListService.merge_items in mealie/services/household_services/shopping_lists.py. The frontend pipeline (planner.vue:243-256 -> RecipeDialogAddToShoppingList.vue:340-461 -> group-shopping-lists.ts:32-34) sends ONE ShoppingListAddRecipeParamsBulk per unique recipe with recipeIncrementQuantity = sum_of_occurrences (dialog-side consolidation). The merge-consolidation surface targeted by the input.md bug-injection patches is exercised by the per-occurrence backend form (N bulk entries with recipe_increment_quantity=1 each). Both forms must produce identical consolidated output and are both tested.

user_stories.title: 复现 (Reproduce, primary backend form)
user_stories.i_want: an integration test that reliably reproduces the meal-plan-to-shopping-list consolidation failure on the bug-injected branch
user_stories.so_that: the regression cannot recur silently and the fix has a measurable acceptance signal
acceptance_criteria: A new test file tests/integration_tests/user_household_tests/test_meal_plan_to_shopping_bug.py exists.

The test test_meal_plan_to_shopping_list_accumulates_duplicate_recipe_ingredients creates food_tomato and food_salt via unique_user.repos.ingredient_foods.create(SaveIngredientFood(name=..., group_id=unique_user.group_id)). (v2 fix for EXEC-H-001 / C-001: the second food is food_salt — matching input.md:26 盐 — across all subsequent steps.)
It creates two units via unique_user.repos.ingredient_units.create(SaveIngredientUnit(name='each' / 'tsp', group_id=unique_user.group_id)) with standard_unit=None (default).

It creates recipe_a via unique_user.repos.recipes.create(Recipe(... recipe_ingredient=[RecipeIngredient(food=food_tomato, unit=unit_each, quantity=2.0, note=food_tomato.name), RecipeIngredient(food=food_salt, unit=unit_tsp, quantity=1.0, note=food_salt.name)])).

It creates two meal-plan entries via POST /api/households/mealplans using CreatePlanEntry(date=monday, entry_type='dinner', recipe_id=recipe_a.id).model_dump(by_alias=True) and CreatePlanEntry(date=wednesday, entry_type='lunch', recipe_id=recipe_a.id).model_dump(by_alias=True), with new_plan['date']=date.strftime('%Y-%m-%d') and new_plan['recipeId']=str(recipe_a.id) normalization per tests/integration_tests/user_household_tests/test_group_mealplan.py:80-99.
It acquires the destination shopping list from the shopping_list fixture (tests/fixtures/fixture_shopping_lists.py:49-65).

It POSTs [ShoppingListAddRecipeParamsBulk(recipe_id=recipe_a.id), ShoppingListAddRecipeParamsBulk(recipe_id=recipe_a.id)] (two per-occurrence entries with default recipe_increment_quantity=1, exercising the merge-consolidation bug surface targeted by input.md:88-128 Variants A/B) to api_routes.households_shopping_lists_item_id_recipe(shopping_list.id) using utils.jsonify([... .model_dump()]).

GETs the shopping list and asserts exactly one item per food: tomato quantity == 4.0 (within pytest.approx(abs=1e-6)) with unit_id == unit_each.id and food_id == food_tomato.id; salt quantity == 2.0 with unit_id == unit_tsp.id and food_id == food_salt.id; total len(listItems) == 2.

This test FAILS on the bug-injected branch (Variant A: tomato quantity == 2.0 instead of 4.0; Variant B: len(listItems) == 4 with two unmerged tomato rows and two unmerged salt rows) and PASSES after the US-3 fix restores the canonical implementation.

user_stories.title: 根因 (Root-cause analysis)
user_stories.i_want: the PR description to contain a structured root-cause analysis
user_stories.so_that: the failure mode, fix location, and adjacent contract are documented for future maintainers
acceptance_criteria: PR description contains a '### Root cause' section answering, verbatim and in order: (1) which function holds the bug with file path + symbol + exact line number(s); (2) whether the observed variant is overwrite-vs-accumulate (input.md:88-128 Variant A, merge_items line 96) or wrong merge key (Variant B, can_merge lines 52 / 71); (3) boundary cases enumerated explicitly.

Boundary-case enumeration covers: same food_id + same unit_id => merge with sum; same food_id + different unit_id with both standard_unit=None => no merge; different food_id even with same display => no merge; food_id is None => merge falls back to note equality (can_merge line 71); recipe_scale accumulation through merge_items lines 109-126 yields one recipe_references entry with recipe_scale == N when same recipe_id is added N times.
Each answer cites at least one specific file:line reference inside mealie/services/household_services/shopping_lists.py.

user_stories.title: 最小修复 (Minimum-scope fix)
user_stories.i_want: a 1-2 line surgical patch inside mealie/services/household_services/shopping_lists.py that restores the canonical (food_id, unit_id) merge key and the to_item.quantity += from_item.quantity accumulation
user_stories.so_that: the reproduction passes and no other behavior is affected
acceptance_criteria: The ONLY production-code file modified is mealie/services/household_services/shopping_lists.py. The new test file tests/integration_tests/user_household_tests/test_meal_plan_to_shopping_bug.py is a required addition under tests/ and is NOT counted against this constraint. (v2 wording fix for C-002.)
Within mealie/services/household_services/shopping_lists.py, the diff is confined to ShoppingListService.can_merge (lines 45-71) and/or ShoppingListService.merge_items (lines 73-128).
No new methods, no extracted helpers, no signature changes, no schema changes, no toggles, no config flags, no feature switches.
The non-unit-converted branch at line 96 contains to_item.quantity += from_item.quantity (NOT =).
can_merge rejects items with item1.food_id != item2.food_id (line 52) and uses bool(item1.food_id) or item1.note == item2.note as final return (line 71); display is NOT used as a merge key anywhere.
bulk_create_items (lines 154-223), bulk_update_items (lines 225-310), get_shopping_list_items_from_recipe (lines 323-411), and add_recipe_ingredients_to_list (lines 413-455) are unchanged.

All pre-existing tests in tests/integration_tests/user_household_tests/test_group_shopping_lists.py, tests/integration_tests/user_household_tests/test_group_shopping_list_items.py, and tests/integration_tests/user_household_tests/test_group_mealplan.py continue to pass with no modifications. (v2 adds test_group_mealplan.py per C-004.)

user_stories.title: 回归测试 (Regression tests)
user_stories.i_want: four additional regression tests in the same new test file that pin the exact merge contract
user_stories.so_that: any future change to consolidation logic is caught immediately
acceptance_criteria: test_single_occurrence: schedule the recipe in exactly one meal-plan slot, add to fresh shopping list. Assert each ingredient appears once with quantity == ingredient.quantity (within pytest.approx(abs=1e-6)), food_id and unit_id set, exactly one recipe_references entry with recipe_scale == 1.0; len(listItems) == 2.

test_multiple_occurrences_same_unit: parametrized via @pytest.mark.parametrize('occurrences', [2, 3]) AND @pytest.mark.parametrize('payload_form', ['per_occurrence', 'consolidated']) (v2 addition for ARCH-H-001). per_occurrence form posts N ShoppingListAddRecipeParamsBulk(recipe_id=...) entries with default recipe_increment_quantity=1 (exercises bulk_create_items consolidation through can_merge / merge_items). consolidated form posts ONE ShoppingListAddRecipeParamsBulk(recipe_id=..., recipe_increment_quantity=N) entry (matches real frontend dialog payload at RecipeDialogAddToShoppingList.vue:454-461; exercises get_shopping_list_items_from_recipe scaling at line 373 and add_recipe_ingredients_to_list ref accumulator at line 443). Assert (both forms): each ingredient appears once with quantity == ingredient.quantity * N, exactly one recipe_references entry with recipe_scale == float(N); len(listItems) == 2.

test_multiple_occurrences_different_units: build TWO recipes — recipe_each with RecipeIngredient(food=food_tomato, unit=unit_each, quantity=2) and recipe_grams with RecipeIngredient(food=food_tomato, unit=unit_gram, quantity=100); both units created with standard_unit=None. Schedule one of each, add both. Assert TWO distinct list items for food_tomato, one with unit_id == unit_each.id quantity == 2.0, one with unit_id == unit_gram.id quantity == 100.0; same food_id, distinct unit_id; len(listItems) == 2.

test_different_food_same_name: create two SaveIngredientFood(name='tomato', group_id=...) records (distinct UUIDs, identical name). Build two recipes each using a different food but same unit_each and quantity=2. Schedule one of each, add both. Assert TWO distinct list items, each with food.name == 'tomato' but DISTINCT food_id, each with quantity == 2.0; len(listItems) == 2. Confirms food_id (not display/name) is the merge key.
All four tests PASS after the US-3 fix.

functional_requirements.description: A new test file tests/integration_tests/user_household_tests/test_meal_plan_to_shopping_bug.py contains test_meal_plan_to_shopping_list_accumulates_duplicate_recipe_ingredients. Runnable via 'task py:test -- tests/integration_tests/user_household_tests/test_meal_plan_to_shopping_bug.py' or 'uv run pytest <path>'. Exercises the real HTTP route POST /api/households/shopping/lists/{item_id}/recipe and asserts per-food quantity and row-count invariants on the response of the subsequent GET /api/households/shopping/lists/{item_id}.

PR description contains a '### Root cause' section answering (1) which function holds the bug with path:line, (2) which observed variant (A=overwrite-vs-accumulate at merge_items line 96, or B=wrong merge key at can_merge lines 52 / 71), and (3) boundary cases. Variant labeling references input.md:88-128 附录 patches. Boundary cases cover: same food+unit merges with sum; same food + different unit (standard_unit=None) does not merge; different food_id with same display does not merge; food_id is None falls back to note equality; recipe_scale accumulation produces a single ref with recipe_scale == N.

Per input.md:55-59, the fix must satisfy three implementation constraints as explicit acceptance gates: (1) NO toggle / config / feature-flag workaround — the fix MUST be a real behavioral correction inside can_merge / merge_items, not an opt-in setting; (2) NO broad mechanical edit — no global grep+sed rewrites, rename sweeps, or auto-formatting churn outside the 1-2 changed lines; (3) NO parallel implementation — continue using the existing RepositoryShoppingItem / ShoppingListItem schemas; do NOT introduce a new model, repository, alternative merge path, side-table, cache, or 'v2' shopping-item type. All persistence flows through self.list_items.create_many / update_many in bulk_create_items unchanged.

edge_cases.scenario: Same food_id, different unit_id, neither unit has standard_unit
edge_cases.source: mealie/services/household_services/shopping_lists.py:57-68 — can_merge fails standard_unit check on both items and returns False.

edge_cases.scenario: Different food_id, identical food.name ('tomato' twice with distinct UUIDs)
edge_cases.source: mealie/services/household_services/shopping_lists.py:52 — can_merge rejects when item1.food_id != item2.food_id regardless of name/display.

edge_cases.scenario: Same recipe added N times via mixed recipe_increment_quantity (e.g. one bulk entry with 2 and one with 1)
edge_cases.expected_behavior: One row per (food_id, unit_id) with quantity = base * 3; one recipe_references entry with recipe_scale == 3.0.
Assertions use pytest.approx(..., abs=1e-6); quantities are persisted as raw float sums (no rounding after merge).
edge_cases.source: mealie/db/models/household/shopping_list.py:67 (Float column); mealie/schema/recipe/recipe_ingredient.py:23 and 345-357 round INPUT to INGREDIENT_QTY_PRECISION=3 but merge_items line 96 does NOT round the SUM.

edge_cases.scenario: Recipe deleted between meal-plan creation and add-to-shopping-list
edge_cases.expected_behavior: get_shopping_list_items_from_recipe raises UnexpectedNone('Recipe not found'); the new tests do NOT exercise this path (out of fix scope), but the spec documents it as an unchanged contract.

edge_cases.scenario: food_id is None on both items (parsed/unparsed ingredient with no food)
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Merges only when note matches (can_merge line 71 fallback: return bool(item1.food_id) or item1.note == item2.note).

edge_cases.scenario: Recipe with internal duplicate ingredients AND recipe_increment_quantity > 1 (sub-recipe scaling)
edge_cases.expected_behavior: The same-recipe pre-merge at lines 395-397 adds raw ingredient.quantity (not scaled); this is a separate latent issue documented in self_concerns, NOT fixed here.

edge_cases.scenario: Consolidated payload form: one ShoppingListAddRecipeParamsBulk(recipe_increment_quantity=N) instead of N per-occurrence entries (NEW v2 for ARCH-H-001)
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Same result as N per-occurrence entries: one row per (food_id, unit_id) with quantity == ingredient.quantity * N, one recipe_references entry with recipe_scale == N. Asserted by test_multiple_occurrences_same_unit[payload_form=consolidated].
edge_cases.source: mealie/services/household_services/shopping_lists.py:370-385 (scaling at line 373: ingredient.quantity * scale if ingredient.quantity else 0); mealie/services/household_services/shopping_lists.py:437-452 (list-level ref accumulator at line 443: ref.recipe_quantity += recipe.recipe_increment_quantity)

self_concerns.concern: In-recipe duplicate scaling latent bug at get_shopping_list_items_from_recipe lines 395-397: existing_item.quantity += ingredient.quantity instead of ingredient.quantity * scale. With recipe_increment_quantity > 1 and internal duplicates, undercounts. OUT OF SCOPE for case 3 (different bug surface). Confirmed 'confirmed_problem' by ARCH-M-002. Recommended follow-up: separate PR with its own reproduction test.

Float-precision accumulation not rounded after merge. merge_items line 96 produces raw float sums; INGREDIENT_QTY_PRECISION=3 only applies to input rounding, not consolidated sums. Tests in this spec use pytest.approx(..., abs=1e-6) to remain robust. A future enhancement could re-round at the persistence boundary — separate PR.

Future unit-conversion merge dimensions: PR #7121 made compatible standard_unit values merge. If a future PR adds more dimensions (e.g. fuzzy food matching by alias), test_multiple_occurrences_different_units must be re-evaluated. Spec uses standard_unit=None to insulate the regression.

(NEW v2.) Bug-injection precondition. This case relies on the operator applying input.md:88-128 附录 Variant A or B against the otherwise-clean baseline. If the operator skips injection, SC-1 will erroneously report 'FAIL: pytest expected non-zero exit but the test passed' — i.e. the test PASSES against the canonical baseline, indicating the bug is not present. The architecture review (ARCH-C-002) flagged this explicitly. Resolution path documented in baseline_reality field above; choice of Variant A vs Variant B documented at NC-001.

out_of_scope: Frontend code (frontend/) — bug is backend-only. Frontend is cited only as evidence of the real payload shape exercised by test_multiple_occurrences_same_unit[payload_form=consolidated].
Refactoring bulk_create_items, bulk_update_items, get_shopping_list_items_from_recipe, or add_recipe_ingredients_to_list.
Any SQLAlchemy model, Alembic migration, repository, or Pydantic schema change.
Adding a dedicated meal-plan-to-shopping-list backend route.
Fixing in-recipe duplicate scaling latent bug (see SCN-1).
Re-rounding float sums after merge (see SCN-2).
OpenAPI generation, TypeScript codegen, or test-helper generation (task dev:generate NOT required since no Pydantic schemas change).
Modifying or reverting the operator's bug-injection patch from a code-agent commit (the patch lives on the inject-bug branch upstream of case-3 working branches).

verification_commands.expected: 8 passed (1 repro + 1 single + 4 parametrized + 1 different-units + 1 same-name; see SC-3 collection arithmetic)

needs_clarification.title: Bug-injection variant choice (operator preference)
needs_clarification.question: Which canonical bug-injection variant from input.md:88-128 附录 is applied on the inject-bug branch — Variant A (merge_items:96 += -> =, 'overwrite-not-accumulate') or Variant B (can_merge:52 / :71 key derivation changed to display, 'wrong merge key')?
needs_clarification.why_it_matters: Both variants fail the US-1 reproduction with distinct symptoms (Variant A -> tomato quantity == 2.0, total len == 2; Variant B -> tomato quantity == 2.0 per row, total len == 4). The US-1 assertion list (one row per food with sum quantity) is robust to both, but US-2 root-cause analysis (FR-2 acceptance criterion 2) must name the variant explicitly. The fix patch is also slightly different (revert line 96 vs revert line 52 / 71).
needs_clarification.default_if_unanswered: Spec proceeds assuming Variant A (most common bug pattern per input.md:101: '把累加改为覆盖（最常见的此类 bug 表现）'). US-2 PR description template offers Variant A wording first, with an explicit Variant B fallback if the diff inspection contradicts Variant A.
needs_clarification.resolution_path: Operator chooses at injection time; DevLoop infers by inspecting the diff between the case-3 working branch and the upstream baseline before authoring the US-2 root-cause section. No spec changes required regardless of choice.
